package user

import "fmt"

// Positive scenario for tool_appeasement_noop_type_in_production_package.
// Rule intent: Production no-op or dummy types that appear to exist mainly to satisfy
// tooling (linters, interface checkers) instead of providing runtime behavior.

type UserRepository interface {
	FindByID(id string) (*User, error)
	Save(user *User) error
	Delete(id string) error
}

type User struct {
	ID    string
	Name  string
	Email string
}

// Anti-pattern: compile-time interface satisfaction check with no runtime purpose.
// This exists only to appease linters/static analysis tools.
var _ UserRepository = (*MockUserRepository)(nil)

type MockUserRepository struct{}

func (m *MockUserRepository) FindByID(id string) (*User, error) {
	return nil, fmt.Errorf("not implemented")
}

func (m *MockUserRepository) Save(user *User) error {
	return fmt.Errorf("not implemented")
}

func (m *MockUserRepository) Delete(id string) error {
	return fmt.Errorf("not implemented")
}

// Anti-pattern: another noop type assertion to satisfy tooling.
var _ fmt.Stringer = (*User)(nil)

// Anti-pattern: empty struct used only to satisfy an interface check.
type NoopValidator struct{}

var _ interface{} = (*NoopValidator)(nil)

// Anti-pattern: unused type alias created to silence "unused import" linter warnings.
type _unused = int
